exemplar.title: develop a website highlighting unique and unknown sports around the world

detailedExemplarData.title: develop a website highlighting unique and unknown sports around the world

strengths: **Excellent organization**: The Gantt chart clearly maps research, content creation and design phases to deadlines, guiding consistent progress.
**Robust information literacy**: You demonstrate thorough cross-referencing and fact-checking using Wikipedia and Google Scholar, underpinning your contentâs credibility.
**Effective communication**: Your formal yet engaging strategy on the home page attracts users and makes complex information accessible.
**High-level creative thinking**: Incorporating swipe-effect boxes for each sport shows innovative interface design that enhances engagement.
**Integrated skill application**: Linking sport rules and cultural contexts directly to your communication plan demonstrates cohesive use of ATL skills to improve product quality.
weaknesses: **Superficial tool justification**: Your choice of Wix is supported by user ratings alone; evaluating functionality, scalability or customization options would deepen your analysis.
**Lacks concrete feedback examples**: Reflections on communication rely on visitor feedback mentions without specific quotes or insights.
**Missing user-testing evidence**: The creative thinking discussion would benefit from actual user responses to the swipe effectâs effectiveness.
**Limited source-selection insight**: While you cite Google Scholar metrics, explaining how citation counts guided your prioritization would strengthen the information literacy narrative.
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: You apply ATL skills impressivelyâyour organizational tools, rigorous fact-checking and creative design are clear strengths. To elevate this further, enrich your tool evaluations with specific criteria and ground your reflections in concrete feedback and testing data.

strengths: **Strong personal motivation**: Your genuine interest in football and physical education provides a compelling context for the project.
**Clear, comprehensive learning goal**: You effectively link exploration of global sports to cultural diversity and sustainability, giving your website a solid foundational purpose.
**Feasible action plan**: Responsibilities, sequencing and resources are clearly outlined, demonstrating that your project is both realistic and well-structured.
weaknesses: **Vague success criteria**: Terms like âcontent accuracyâ and âaestheticsâ lack measurable benchmarks (e.g. error rate <2%, specific color contrast ratios).
**Limited resource descriptions**: Repeatedly listing only âcomputerâ misses opportunities to specify software tools, tutorials or collaborative support you will need.
**Timeline gaps**: While start dates are noted, clear completion milestones and deadlines for each deliverable are absent, reducing progress-tracking precision.
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your planning shows a strong sense of purpose and a well-sequenced action plan, but sharpening your success criteria with concrete targets, detailing the tools and external supports you will use, and embedding clear deadlines will make your plan more rigorous and easier to monitor.

strengths: **Deep self-awareness**: You articulate how the project enhanced your web design, public speaking and sports knowledge, showing genuine personal growth.
**Evidence-based engagement insights**: Referencing survey percentages and linking features like click-to-play videos to user engagement demonstrates strong use of data.
**Transparent product evaluation**: Structuring your self-assessment directly against rubric criteria makes your judgments clear and traceable.
**Honest technical reflection**: Acknowledging minor mobile-responsiveness issues, supported by peer feedback, illustrates reflective accuracy.
weaknesses: **Superficial aesthetics review**: Focusing only on positive feedback ignores the 22% of mid-range responses and misses opportunities for design improvement.
**Incomplete navigation reflection**: Noting a peerâs suggestion for a search bar without outlining how you might implement it limits the growth-mindset evident in other sections.
**Missed broader feedback**: Concluding remarks discount external suggestions rather than exploring how they could enrich future iterations.
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your reflective work is structured and insightful, using concrete data to support your claims. By engaging more thoroughly with constructive criticismâespecially around aesthetics, navigation features and broad user suggestionsâyou can demonstrate an even stronger commitment to continuous improvement.

annotations.comment: Use of a Gantt chart to link deadlines to research, content creation, and web design shows strong Organizational skill guiding consistent progress.
Resource descriptions repeatedly list only âcomputerâ; identify additional software tutorials or collaboration needs to strengthen your planning.
Your personal interest in football and physical education is effectively connected to the project focus, demonstrating genuine motivation and context for your learning goal.
You present a clear, comprehensive learning goal linking exploration of global sports to cultural diversity and sustainability, establishing strong foundational purpose for your website.

Success criteria like âContent accuracyâ and âAestheticsâ are defined but lack measurable targets; consider specifying precise benchmarks (e.g. error rate <2%, consistent color contrast ratios).
Timeline entries note start dates but omit specific completion milestones; adding clear deadlines for each deliverable will enhance progress tracking.
Your action plan outlines research steps with responsibilities, timeline, and resources, demonstrating feasibility, clear sequencing, and resource awareness.
Detailed example of cross-referencing and fact-checking through Wikipedia and Google Scholar demonstrates robust Information Literacy supporting content credibility.
Your formal yet engaging communication strategy on the home page effectively attracts users and clarifies complex information, showing strong Communication skills.
Incorporation of swipe-effect boxes for each sport illustrates creative interface design, reflecting high-level Creative Thinking to enhance user engagement.
When justifying Wix selection, include evaluative criteria beyond user ratingsâlike functionality or scalabilityâto deepen your Information Literacy analysis.
Your Communication reflection mentions visitor feedback but would be stronger with specific quotes or examples to substantiate impact.
Your reflection clearly articulates the projectâs impact on your learningâenhancing web design, public speaking, and sports knowledgeâdemonstrating deep self-awareness.
User Engagement reflection is evidence-based, referencing survey percentages and linking features like click-to-play videos to engagement outcomes.
Creative Thinking discussion could be bolstered by citing user testing feedback on the swipe effectâs effectiveness.
Information Literacy description introduces Google Scholar metrics; adding a brief example of how citation counts guided source prioritization would deepen insight.
Linking exploration of rules and cultural contexts for each sport to your communication plan exemplifies integration of ATL skills into product quality.
Structured self-evaluation against rubric levels provides a transparent Product evaluation framework and shows strong use of success criteria.
In your Aesthetics evaluation, discuss the 22% of mid-range responses to identify design areas for refinement rather than focusing solely on positives.
Your Mobile Responsiveness section honestly acknowledges minor distortions, supported by peer feedback, illustrating reflective depth and accuracy.
Navigation reflection notes peer suggestion for a search bar; reflecting on how you might integrate this feature would demonstrate a growth mindset.
Concluding remarks dismiss broader feedback; considering audience suggestions beyond your criteria could enrich future iterations and show openness to improvement.
